task_completion.visual_evidence: The left sidebar or folder list in BlueMail displays the three newly created folders: 'Incident-Infrastructure', 'Incident-Security', and 'Incident-Software'.

task_completion.description: Move at least five relevant emails from the inbox into the newly created incident folders.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The incident folders show a non-zero email count, or their contents are visible in screenshots, totaling at least 5 emails across the three folders.

task_completion.description: Prepare a draft email to infra-alerts@company.com with 'Infrastructure' in the subject and placeholders in the body.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A draft email window or entry in the Drafts folder addressed to infra-alerts@company.com, with 'Infrastructure' in the subject line and placeholder text (e.g., 'affected', 'recovery') in the body.

task_completion.description: Prepare a draft email to security-team@company.com with 'Security' in the subject and placeholders in the body.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A draft email window or entry in the Drafts folder addressed to security-team@company.com, with 'Security' in the subject line and placeholder text in the body.

task_completion.description: Prepare a draft email to engineering@company.com with 'Software' in the subject and placeholders in the body.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A draft email window or entry in the Drafts folder addressed to engineering@company.com, with 'Software' in the subject line and placeholder text in the body.

task_completion.description: Draft a final confirmation email to cto@company.com stating that the folders and templates are ready.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A draft email window or entry in the Drafts folder addressed to cto@company.com, with a body confirming that the folders and templates are ready.

integrity.description: The agent used the BlueMail GUI to create folders, move emails, and draft emails.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the BlueMail application interface being actively used (e.g., compose windows, folder creation dialogs, drag-and-drop actions).

integrity.description: The moved emails are genuine emails from the inbox, not newly composed fake emails.
integrity.visual_evidence: The emails in the incident folders appear to be real emails from the inbox (e.g., dated August 2002, from mailing lists), rather than newly created messages.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows showing sqlite3 commands or file manipulations directly altering the BlueMail database or mail directories.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The inbox initially contains 50 real ham emails from August 2002 (SpamAssassin public corpus). Required folders are exactly: 'Incident-Infrastructure', 'Incident-Security', 'Incident-Software'. At least 5 emails must be moved in total. Required draft recipients are infra-alerts@company.com, security-team@company.com, engineering@company.com, and cto@company.com. Acceptable body placeholders include: 'affected', 'estimated', 'recovery', 'threat', 'scope', 'workaround', 'error', 'escalation'. The CTO draft subject must contain 'Emergency', 'Incident', or 'Communication'.
